div1.entryFree:
Vicious, 1) devoted to vice, morally corrupt: Err. IV, 2, 21. H6A V, 4, 35. H8 I, 2, 117. Tim. IV, 3, 213. Lr. I, 1, 230. V, 3, 172.
2) defective, faulty: for some v. mole of nature in them, Hml. I, 4, 24.
3) wrong: though I perchance am v. in my guess, as, I confess, it is my nature's plague to spy into abuses, Oth. III, 3, 145. it had been v. to have mistrusted her, Cymb. V, 5, 65 (== blameable).
